title: Respect Helm values precedence when layering overrides
description: Order overrides knowingly: chart defaults < parent values < -f files (last wins) < --set, and avoid deep-key collisions that silently lose values.
body: Use when a value override is not taking effect or you are layering environment configs. Do not assume the chart's values.yaml wins or that multiple -f files merge by union (later files override earlier on conflicting keys). Actions: remember precedence is subchart/chart defaults, then parent values.yaml, then each -f file in order, then --set highest; render with helm template --debug to see the effective value; avoid setting the same deep key in two layers expecting a merge. Validation signals include helm template showing the expected value and a diff confirming only intended keys changed. Risk boundary: changing values that drive replica count or resource limits on a production release is human-gated.
